findings.description: The source contains a note inside the tempDirectory default-value cell explaining Jetty's actual behavior: `./work` is used as the JSP compile directory; if `./work` does not exist, the system Temp folder (on Windows: user home/Local Settings/Temp) is used instead. The knowledge file only records the default value as 「jettyのデフォルト動作に依存」 without this detail.
findings.source_evidence: tempDirectory セルのnote: 「jettyのデフォルト動作では、./workがコンパイル先ディレクトリとなる。./workが存在しない場合は、Tempフォルダ(Windowsの場合は、ユーザのホームディレクトリ/Local Settings/Temp)が出力先となる。」

findings.description: The source file contains `-Xverfiy:none` (a typo — 'fiy' instead of 'ify'). The knowledge file renders this as `-Xverify:none` (the correct spelling). While this is an obvious source typo, the knowledge file text does not match the source literally.
